impact: The repeated failures are syntactic, not domain-contract failures. This repair gives the daemon a deterministic recovery protocol: constrain generated Python to simple unittest code, require py_compile before unittest discovery, keep one mutation per test, and avoid broad validator rewrites. That should let the worker resume meaningful progress without touching PP&D application artifacts or extractor implementation code.
summary: Add a narrow supervisor repair guide for Python SyntaxError recovery on checkbox-27 style validator tasks.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 1 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-27: Recovery for checkbox-27: add the one-file mutation validator for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_expected_output.json` using only syntactically simple Python, standard-library `unittest`, exact fixture loading, an initial committed-fixture-valid assertion, one mutation per test, and an explicit `py_compile`-first self-check. Cover missing `categoryId`, missing `sourceEvidenceIds`, missing local `evidence[].evidenceId`, out-of-range `confidence`, duplicate `(categoryId, action, object, sourceEvidenceIds)` tuples, and malformed stop-gate classification. Do not add extractor implementation code.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
